params.text:
Welcome to Diabetes Awareness

Diabetes is one of the most common long-term conditions you will meet in adult social care. Many of the people you support will be living with it, and the everyday care you give — around meals, medication reminders, activity and simply noticing when someone is unwell — makes a real difference to how well they stay. This course builds your awareness so you can support people confidently and know when to act.

  • Understand: what diabetes is and the main types.
  • Recognise: the signs of a hypo and a hyper.
  • Respond: knowing what to do and when to call for help.

What this course covers

Over the next pages you will learn how the body uses glucose, the difference between Type 1 and Type 2 diabetes, why good control matters, and how to recognise and respond to the two main emergencies — hypoglycaemia and hyperglycaemia. You will also see how to support people with diabetes in their daily lives. It is grounded in NICE guidance, Diabetes UK information and NHS good practice.

This is an awareness and knowledge course. It helps you support people and recognise problems, but it does not authorise you to administer insulin or any diabetes medication. That may only be done with specific training, assessed competence and a proper delegation from a qualified professional. You do not diagnose diabetes — that is a clinical decision.
